Patch Set 1:

By the way, this issue crops up with the testing of 'PAUSING' in multiop_bg_pause in test-framework.sh.  I spent some time trying to improve that and I couldn't find an easy way - the backgrounding makes it tricky.

If someone (Andreas, heh) has a way to improve that so it could capture all of the multiop output and look for PAUSING in it, that would be a better fix.  With the backgrounding, I couldn't figure out how to gather all the output *and* not end up waiting the full timeout every time...  So I took the easy route of fixing 185, which you see here.  (All other uses of multiop_bg_pause look OK)

commit.subject: LU-17019 tests: fix 'v' usage in test 185
commit.message:
LU-17019 tests: fix 'v' usage in test 185

Test 185 provides 'v' to multiop when using the
multiop_bg_pause function, which already adds 'v' to the
multiop command.  Using a verbosity level > 2 breaks the
code which checks the multiop output for PAUSING, now that
there is other text in that output.

Fixes: 84376bf674 ("LU-13805 tests: add unaligned io to multiop")
Signed-off-by: Patrick Farrell <pfarrell@whamcloud.com>
Change-Id: I7023e47956e32a3f681fec504a1fc9200dbbf24f
Reviewed-on: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/51898
Tested-by: jenkins <devops@whamcloud.com>
Reviewed-by: Andreas Dilger <adilger@whamcloud.com>
